TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit ConstructorBuilder extends System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo
Public MustInherit Class ConstructorBuilder Inherits ConstructorInfo
public ref class ConstructorBuilder abstract : System::Reflection::ConstructorInfo

Docs.summary: Defines and represents a constructor of a dynamic class.

remarks.format:
## Remarks <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder> is used to fully describe a constructor in Microsoft intermediate language (MSIL), including the name, attributes, signature, and constructor body. It is used in conjunction with the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder> class to create classes at run time. Call <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.DefineConstructor*> to get an instance of <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder>.
If you do not define a constructor for your dynamic type, a parameterless constructor is provided automatically, and it calls the parameterless constructor of the base class.
If you use <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder> to define a constructor for your dynamic type, a parameterless constructor is not provided. You have the following options for providing a parameterless constructor in addition to the constructor you defined:
- If you want a parameterless constructor that simply calls the parameterless constructor of the base class, you can use the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.DefineDefaultConstructor*?displayProperty=nameWithType> method to create one (and optionally restrict access to it). Do not provide an implementation for this parameterless constructor. If you do, an exception is thrown when you try to use the constructor. No exception is thrown when the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.CreateType*?displayProperty=nameWithType> method is called.
- If you want a parameterless constructor that does something more than simply calling the parameterless constructor of the base class, or that calls another constructor of the base class, or that does something else entirely, you must use the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.DefineConstructor*?displayProperty=nameWithType> method to create a <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder>, and provide your own implementation.
## Examples The following code sample illustrates the contextual usage of a `ConstructorBuilder`.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/Overview/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/Overview/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: protected ConstructorBuilder ();
.method familyhidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed
Protected Sub New ()
protected: ConstructorBuilder();

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
Docs.remarks: This constructor is invoked by derived classes.

Docs.summary: Gets a reference to the object for the type that declares this member.
Docs.value: The type that declares this member.

remarks.format:
## Remarks A member of a class (or interface) is either declared or inherited from a base class (or interface).
The following code sample illustrates the use of `DeclaringType`.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/AddDeclarativeSecurity/constructorbuilder_attributes_4.cs" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/AddDeclarativeSecurity/constructorbuilder_attributes_4.vb" id="Snippet2":::

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder DefineParameter (int iSequence, System.Reflection.ParameterAttributes attributes, string? strParamName);

.method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder DefineParameter(int32 iSequence, valuetype System.Reflection.ParameterAttributes attributes, string strParamName) cil managed
Public Function DefineParameter (iSequence As Integer, attributes As ParameterAttributes, strParamName As String) As ParameterBuilder
member this.DefineParameter : int * System.Reflection.ParameterAttributes * string -> System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder
public: System::Reflection::Emit::ParameterBuilder ^ DefineParameter(int iSequence, System::Reflection::ParameterAttributes attributes, System::String ^ strParamName);

Docs.param: The position of the parameter in the parameter list. Parameters are indexed beginning with the number 1 for the first parameter.
The attributes of the parameter.
The name of the parameter. The name can be the null string.
Docs.summary: Defines a parameter of this constructor.
Docs.returns: An object that represents the new parameter of this constructor.

remarks.format: ## Remarks If you specify 0 (zero) for `iSequence`, this method returns a <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder> instead of throwing an exception. There is nothing useful that you can do with this <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder>.

Docs.exception: is less than 0 (zero), or it is greater than the number of parameters of the constructor.
The containing type has been created using .

MemberSignature.Value: protected abstract System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder DefineParameterCore (int iSequence, System.Reflection.ParameterAttributes attributes, string strParamName);

.method familyhidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder DefineParameterCore(int32 iSequence, valuetype System.Reflection.ParameterAttributes attributes, string strParamName) cil managed
Protected MustOverride Function DefineParameterCore (iSequence As Integer, attributes As ParameterAttributes, strParamName As String) As ParameterBuilder
abstract member DefineParameterCore : int * System.Reflection.ParameterAttributes * string -> System.Reflection.Emit.ParameterBuilder
protected: abstract System::Reflection::Emit::ParameterBuilder ^ DefineParameterCore(int iSequence, System::Reflection::ParameterAttributes attributes, System::String ^ strParamName);

Docs.param: The position of the parameter in the parameter list. Parameters are indexed beginning with the number 1 for the first parameter.
The attributes of the parameter.
The name of the parameter. The name can be the string.
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, defines a parameter of this constructor.
Docs.returns: A that represents the new parameter of this constructor.

Docs.summary: Returns the custom attributes defined for this constructor.

MemberSignature.Value: public override object[] GetCustomAttributes (bool inherit);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance object[] GetCustomAttributes(bool inherit) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetCustomAttributes (inherit As Boolean) As Object()
override this.GetCustomAttributes : bool -> obj[]
public: override cli::array <System::Object ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(bool inherit);

Docs.param: Controls inheritance of custom attributes from base classes. This parameter is ignored.
Docs.summary: Returns all the custom attributes defined for this constructor.
Docs.returns: An array of objects representing all the custom attributes of the constructor represented by this instance.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The `inherit` parameter is ignored because a class never inherits constructors from base classes.
To get the custom attributes, finish building the type by calling <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.CreateType*>, retrieve the constructor by calling the <xref:System.Type.GetConstructor*> method on the returned type, and then call the <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo.GetCustomAttributes*> method on the returned <xref:System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo>.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported.

MemberSignature.Value: public override object[] GetCustomAttributes (Type attributeType, bool inherit);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance object[] GetCustomAttributes(class System.Type attributeType, bool inherit) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetCustomAttributes (attributeType As Type, inherit As Boolean) As Object()
override this.GetCustomAttributes : Type * bool -> obj[]
public: override cli::array <System::Object ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(Type ^ attributeType, bool inherit);

Docs.param: The custom attribute type.
Controls inheritance of custom attributes from base classes. This parameter is ignored.
Docs.summary: Returns the custom attributes identified by the given type.
Docs.returns: An object array that represents the attributes of this constructor.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The `inherit` parameter is ignored because a class never inherits constructors from base classes.
To get the custom attributes, finish building the type by calling <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.CreateType*>, retrieve the constructor by calling the <xref:System.Type.GetConstructor*> method on the returned type, and then call the <xref:System.Reflection.MemberInfo.GetCustomAttributes*> method on the returned <xref:System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo>.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported.

Docs.summary: Gets an that can be used to emit a method body for this constructor.

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator GetILGenerator ();
.method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator GetILGenerator() cil managed
Public Function GetILGenerator () As ILGenerator
member this.GetILGenerator : unit -> System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator
public: System::Reflection::Emit::ILGenerator ^ GetILGenerator();

Docs.summary: Gets an for this constructor.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The runtime generates the code for default constructors. Therefore, if an attempt is made to obtain an `ILGenerator`, an exception will be thrown.
## Examples The following code sample illustrates the use of the `GetILGenerator` method.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/Overview/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/Overview/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception:
The constructor is a parameterless constructor.
-or-
The constructor has or flags indicating that it should not have a method body.

MemberSignature.Value: public System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator GetILGenerator (int streamSize);
.method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator GetILGenerator(int32 streamSize) cil managed
Public Function GetILGenerator (streamSize As Integer) As ILGenerator
member this.GetILGenerator : int -> System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator
public: System::Reflection::Emit::ILGenerator ^ GetILGenerator(int streamSize);

Docs.param: The size of the MSIL stream, in bytes.
Docs.summary: Gets an object, with the specified MSIL stream size, that can be used to build a method body for this constructor.

remarks.format: ## Remarks The runtime generates the code for default constructors. Therefore, if an attempt is made to obtain an `ILGenerator` for a parameterless constructor, an exception is thrown.

Docs.exception:
The constructor is a parameterless constructor.
-or-
The constructor has or flags indicating that it should not have a method body.

Docs.value: Read/write. Gets or sets whether the local variables in this constructor should be zero-initialized.

.property instance bool InitLocalsCore
Protected MustOverride Property InitLocalsCore As Boolean
member this.InitLocalsCore : bool with get, set

Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, gets or sets a value that indicates whether the local variables in this constructor should be zero-initialized.
Docs.value: if the local variables in this constructor are initialized to 0; otherwise, .

Docs.summary: Invokes the constructor dynamically reflected by this instance.

MemberSignature.Value: public override object Invoke (System.Reflection.BindingFlags invokeAttr, System.Reflection.Binder? binder, object?[]? parameters, System.Globalization.CultureInfo? culture);

.method public hidebysig virtual instance object Invoke(valuetype System.Reflection.BindingFlags invokeAttr, class System.Reflection.Binder binder, object[] parameters, class System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture) cil managed
Public Overrides Function Invoke (invokeAttr As BindingFlags, binder As Binder, parameters As Object(), culture As CultureInfo) As Object
override this.Invoke : System.Reflection.BindingFlags * System.Reflection.Binder * obj[] * System.Globalization.CultureInfo -> obj

public: override System::Object ^ Invoke(System::Reflection::BindingFlags invokeAttr, System::Reflection::Binder ^ binder, cli::array <System::Object ^> ^ parameters, System::Globalization::CultureInfo ^ culture);

Docs.param: This must be a bit flag from , such as InvokeMethod, NonPublic, and so on.
An object that enables the binding, coercion of argument types, invocation of members, and retrieval of objects using reflection. If binder is , the default binder is used. See .
An argument list. This is an array of arguments with the same number, order, and type as the parameters of the constructor to be invoked. If there are no parameters this should be .

An instance of used to govern the coercion of types. If this is null, the for the current thread is used. (For example, this is necessary to convert a that represents 1000 to a value, since 1000 is represented differently by different cultures.)
Docs.summary: Dynamically invokes the constructor represented by this instance on the given object, passing along the specified parameters, and under the constraints of the given binder.
Docs.returns: The value returned by the invoked constructor.

remarks.format:
## Remarks If the constructor has no parameters, the value of the `parameters` parameter should be `null`. Otherwise, the number, type, and order of elements in the `parameters` array should be identical to the number, type, and order of parameters for the constructor reflected by this instance.
For example, if the constructor reflected by this instance is declared as public class taking two strings, then the `parameters` parameter should be an array of <xref:System.Object> with length 2.
> [!NOTE] > Access restrictions are ignored for fully-trusted code. That is, private constructors, methods, fields, and properties can be accessed and invoked using Reflection whenever the code is fully trusted.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported. You can retrieve the constructor using and call on the returned .

MemberSignature.Value: public override object Invoke (object? obj, System.Reflection.BindingFlags invokeAttr, System.Reflection.Binder? binder, object?[]? parameters, System.Globalization.CultureInfo? culture);

.method public hidebysig virtual instance object Invoke(object obj, valuetype System.Reflection.BindingFlags invokeAttr, class System.Reflection.Binder binder, object[] parameters, class System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture) cil managed
Public Overrides Function Invoke (obj As Object, invokeAttr As BindingFlags, binder As Binder, parameters As Object(), culture As CultureInfo) As Object
override this.Invoke : obj * System.Reflection.BindingFlags * System.Reflection.Binder * obj[] * System.Globalization.CultureInfo -> obj

public: override System::Object ^ Invoke(System::Object ^ obj, System::Reflection::BindingFlags invokeAttr, System::Reflection::Binder ^ binder, cli::array <System::Object ^> ^ parameters, System::Globalization::CultureInfo ^ culture);

Docs.param: The object that needs to be reinitialized.
One of the values that specifies the type of binding that is desired.
A that defines a set of properties and enables the binding, coercion of argument types, and invocation of members using reflection. If is , then Binder.DefaultBinding is used.

An argument list. This is an array of arguments with the same number, order, and type as the parameters of the constructor to be invoked. If there are no parameters, this should be a null reference ( in Visual Basic).
A used to govern the coercion of types. If this is null, the for the current thread is used.
Docs.summary: Dynamically invokes the constructor reflected by this instance with the specified arguments, under the constraints of the specified .
Docs.returns: An instance of the class associated with the constructor.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The number, type, and order of elements in the parameters array should be identical to the number, type, and order of parameters for the constructor reflected by this instance.
Before calling the constructor, `Invoke` ensures that the caller has access permission, and that the parameters are of the correct number, order and type.
Access restrictions are ignored for fully-trusted code. That is, private constructors, methods, fields, and properties can be accessed and invoked using Reflection whenever the code is fully trusted.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported. You can retrieve the constructor using and call on the returned .

MemberSignature.Value: public override bool IsDefined (Type attributeType, bool inherit);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance bool IsDefined(class System.Type attributeType, bool inherit) cil managed
Public Overrides Function IsDefined (attributeType As Type, inherit As Boolean) As Boolean
override this.IsDefined : Type * bool -> bool
public: override bool IsDefined(Type ^ attributeType, bool inherit);

Docs.param: A custom attribute type.
Controls inheritance of custom attributes from base classes. This parameter is ignored.
Docs.summary: Checks if the specified custom attribute type is defined.
Docs.returns: if the specified custom attribute type is defined; otherwise, .

remarks.format: ## Remarks See the metadata specification for details on how to format `binaryAttribute`.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported. You can retrieve the constructor using and call on the returned .

MemberSignature.Value: public override int MetadataToken { get; }
.property instance int32 MetadataToken
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property MetadataToken As Integer
member this.MetadataToken : int
public: virtual property int MetadataToken { int get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a token that identifies the current dynamic module in metadata.
Docs.value: An integer token that identifies the current module in metadata.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The tokens obtained using this property can be passed to the unmanaged Reflection API.
> [!NOTE] > Information about metadata tokens can be found in the Common Language Infrastructure (CLI) documentation, especially "Partition II: Metadata Definition and Semantics". For more information, see [ECMA 335 Common Language Infrastructure (CLI)](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-335/).

MemberSignature.Value: public override RuntimeMethodHandle MethodHandle { get; }
.property instance valuetype System.RuntimeMethodHandle MethodHandle
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property MethodHandle As RuntimeMethodHandle
member this.MethodHandle : RuntimeMethodHandle
public: virtual property RuntimeMethodHandle MethodHandle { RuntimeMethodHandle get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the internal handle for the method. Use this handle to access the underlying metadata handle.
Docs.value: The internal handle for the method. Use this handle to access the underlying metadata handle.
Docs.exception: This property is not supported on this class.

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.Reflection.Module Module { get; }
.property instance class System.Reflection.Module Module
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property Module As Module
member this.Module : System.Reflection.Module
public: virtual property System::Reflection::Module ^ Module { System::Reflection::Module ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the dynamic module in which this constructor is defined.
Docs.value: A object that represents the dynamic module in which this constructor is defined.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This property returns the same value as the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder.GetModule*> method.

.property instance string Name
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property Name As String
member this.Name : string
public: virtual property System::String ^ Name { System::String ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Retrieves the name of this constructor.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The following code sample illustrates the use of `Name`.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/Name/constructorbuilder_name_5.cs" id="Snippet2"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/Name/constructorbuilder_name_5.vb" id="Snippet2":::

MemberSignature.Value: public override Type? ReflectedType { get; }
.property instance class System.Type ReflectedType
Public Overrides ReadOnly Property ReflectedType As Type
member this.ReflectedType : Type
public: virtual property Type ^ ReflectedType { Type ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Holds a reference to the object from which this object was obtained.
Docs.value: The object from which this object was obtained.

remarks.format: ## Remarks A <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder> object represents a constructor on a particular class. In order to obtain a <xref:System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo> object, the <xref:System.Type> object that represents the class that supports the constructor is queried. This property holds a reference to that <xref:System.Type> object.

Docs.summary: Sets a custom attribute.

MemberSignature.Value: public void SetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder customBuilder);
.method public hidebysig instance void SetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder customBuilder) cil managed
Public Sub SetCustomAttribute (customBuilder As CustomAttributeBuilder)
member this.SetCustomAttribute : System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder -> unit
public: void SetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::Emit::CustomAttributeBuilder ^ customBuilder);

Docs.param: An instance of a helper class to define the custom attribute.
Docs.summary: Set a custom attribute using a custom attribute builder.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following code sample illustrates the use of `SetCustomAttribute` of the context of a <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder>, passing a <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/constructorbuilder_setcustomattribute1.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/constructorbuilder_setcustomattribute1.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public void SetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo con, byte[] binaryAttribute);
.method public hidebysig instance void SetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo con, unsigned int8[] binaryAttribute) cil managed
Public Sub SetCustomAttribute (con As ConstructorInfo, binaryAttribute As Byte())
member this.SetCustomAttribute : System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo * byte[] -> unit
public: void SetCustomAttribute(System::Reflection::ConstructorInfo ^ con, cli::array <System::Byte> ^ binaryAttribute);

Docs.param: The constructor for the custom attribute.
A byte blob representing the attributes.
Docs.summary: Set a custom attribute using a specified custom attribute blob.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
For information on how to format `binaryAttribute`, see the metadata specification in Partition II of the [Common Language Infrastructure (CLI) specification](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-335/).
## Examples The following code sample illustrates the use of `SetCustomAttribute` of the context of a <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.ConstructorBuilder>, passing a byte blob.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/constructorbuilder_setcustomattribute2.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/ConstructorBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/constructorbuilder_setcustomattribute2.vb" id="Snippet1":::

abstract member SetCustomAttributeCore : System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo * ReadOnlySpan<byte> -> unit
protected: abstract void SetCustomAttributeCore(System::Reflection::ConstructorInfo ^ con, ReadOnlySpan<System::Byte> binaryAttribute);

Docs.param: The constructor for the custom attribute.
A of bytes representing the attribute.
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, sets a custom attribute on this constructor.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string ToString ();
.method public hidebysig virtual instance string ToString() cil managed
Public Overrides Function ToString () As String
override this.ToString : unit -> string
public: override System::String ^ ToString();

Docs.summary: Returns this instance as a .
Docs.returns: A string containing the name, attributes, and exceptions of this constructor, followed by the current Microsoft intermediate language (MSIL) stream.
